channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Oracle Q4 revenue rises 21% as cloud growth accelerates; FY26 sales and marketing spend falls 4%
item.description: Oracle’s cloud infrastructure business nearly doubled in the quarter, but investors focused on its plans to raise $40 billion and ramp up AI-related spending, sending the stock lower in after-hours trading. [Oracle]

item.title: Oracle spent $55.7 billion on data centres in a single year, beat its own guidance by $5.7 billion, and wants to raise another $40 billion
item.description: Oracle reported fiscal fourth-quarter revenue of $19.2 billion, up 21% year on year, and adjusted earnings of $2.11 per share, beating analyst estimates of $19.1 billion and $1.97 respectively. The numbers were overshadowed by capital expenditure that came in… [Oracle]

item.title: Explained: Why Oracle shares crashed 10% in extended trading despite earnings beat
item.description: Oracle shares fell sharply in after-hours trading despite beating earnings estimates, as investors reacted to aggressive AI spending plans and rising debt concerns. The company’s massive capital raise and infrastructure push raised fears of over-leverage, eve… [Oracle]

item.title: Alphabet vs Microsoft Stocks: Analyst Views on Buy or Sell in 2026
item.description: In 2026, investors face a choice between Alphabet and Microsoft, both leading in AI and cloud computing. Each company offers unique growth drivers and valuation profiles, influencing investment decisions. [Alphabet, Microsoft]

item.title: Oracle’s AI backlog explodes to $638B, up 363% year-over-year, but investors aren’t celebrating
item.description: Oracle's massive AI backlog highlights the high stakes and risks of capital-intensive growth, with investor skepticism over cash flow sustainability. The post Oracle’s AI backlog explodes to $638B, up 363% year-over-year, but investors aren’t celebrating appe… [Oracle]

item.title: What SpaceX’s record IPO really means for the OpenAI and Anthropic listings behind it
item.description: The SpaceX IPO is set to price the biggest stock-market debut in history this week, raising about $75bn at a valuation near $1.75tn, with shares trading on Nasdaq from Friday. But the most consequential thing about Elon Musk’s rocket company going public may … [OpenAI, Anthropic]
